Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.3200  WATCH

Us Equity Stress Canary operates an advanced, deeply integrated supply chain network with deep logistics infrastructure, multiple port redundancies, and sophisticated inventory management — reducing but not eliminating chokepoint sensitivity. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Panama Canal (significant dependency, weight 0.50); Strait of Malacca (moderate dependency, weight 0.30); Suez Canal (moderate dependency, weight 0.25).

Critical import dependencies include semiconductors, rare earth metals, consumer electronics, pharmaceuticals, steel. Semiconductor and critical mineral import dependency exposes the sovereign to US-China technology decoupling and East Asian supply chain disruptions.

Primary exports — semiconductors, pharmaceuticals, financial services, software — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.3200 WATCH — Structural supply chain sensitivity is elevated. Chokepoint and critical import dependencies warrant close monitoring. A single trigger event (Strait closure, export ban, major port strike) could escalate to ELEVATED within days.

Watch signals: Panama Canal closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  Export controls on rare earth metals or semiconductors from China → critical materials shortage  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.2000  NORMAL

Us Equity Stress Canary is classified as a developed real estate market. Primary stress vector is equity market volatility, VIX regime escalation, and breadth deterioration. Commercial real estate exposure is significant CRE concentration with elevated office and retail vacancy risk.

As a developed market, property values are sensitive to interest rate cycles — the aggressive central bank tightening of 2022-2024 has left mortgage holders with elevated refinancing risk. Commercial office markets face structural headwinds from remote work adoption globally.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.2000 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: CMBS delinquency rate rise above 5% → commercial property stress signal  |  Mortgage refinancing cliff (2025-2027 vintage) → household stress spike  |  Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.2700  WATCH

Us Equity Stress Canary faces 4 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Hurricane / Typhoon, Tornado / Hail, Wildfire, Flood. High-severity events (Hurricane / Typhoon, Wildfire) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in is deep and liquid, with well-capitalised domestic insurers and active reinsurance participation. Reinsurance profile: low reinsurance dependency — domestic capacity absorbs most shock.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.2700 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Hurricane / Typhoon event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress
